marketFocus: NVIDIA post-earnings selloff: stock closed -5.5% despite Q4 beat, tech weakness spreads to broader indices (SPX -0.54%, NDX -1.16%)
Tokyo Core CPI fell below BoJ's 2% target for first time since Oct 2024 (1.8% vs 2.0%), yen firmer on data
PBoC cut FX Risk Reserve Ratio to 0% (from 20%) to slow yuan appreciation, CNH weakened on policy shift
US-Iran nuclear talks in Geneva end with no deal but 'significant progress', technical talks resume Monday in Vienna

USD.name: USD
USD.summary: DXY slightly softer in rangebound trade amid mixed performances in major Asian counterparts. Fed's Goolsbee (2027 voter) stated rates can come down but does not want to front-load before inflation eases. Critical US Core PCE (Fed's preferred inflation gauge) and Chicago PMI scheduled today at 14:30 Frankfurt Time.
drivers: DXY rangebound, slightly softer amid mixed risk appetite and tech weakness
Fed's Goolsbee: rates can come down, but don't want to front-load before inflation eases
Fed's Goolsbee: one of the more optimistic folks on Fed about rate cuts this year
Trump-Xi summit preparations faltering, China wants 'many months' process (SCMP)
Today: Core PCE Price Index m/m (exp 0.3% vs prev 0.2%), Chicago PMI (exp 48.5 vs prev 49.2)

EUR.name: EUR
EUR.summary: EUR/USD eked mild gains after oscillating around 1.1800 focal point. European equity futures flat after Thursday's -0.2% close. Participants await CPI data from Germany, France, and Spain, plus German Unemployment Rate. EU Commission eyes legal loophole to bypass Hungary's veto of EUR 90bln loan (FT).
drivers: EUR/USD oscillating around 1.1800 level, mild gains on softer DXY
European equity futures flat (Euro Stoxx 50) after Thursday's -0.2% close
EU Commission eyes legal loophole to bypass Hungary's EUR 90bln loan veto (FT)
Focus on German CPI (Feb), French Prelim CPI (Feb), Spanish Flash CPI (Feb)
Today: German Unemployment Change (exp 2K vs prev 0K), German Import Prices (Jan)

GBP.name: GBP
GBP.summary: GBP/USD languished near 1.3500 level following prior day's underperformance owing to credit concerns (MFS collapse). Overnight price action contained after UK GfK Consumer Confidence fell to -19 (exp -15, prev -16), lowest level since November. UK Green Party won Gorton & Denton by-election, defeating PM Starmer's Labour.
drivers: GBP/USD pressured near 1.3500, credit concerns weigh (MFS collapse)
UK GfK Consumer Confidence fell to -19 (exp -15, prev -16), lowest since November
UK Green Party wins Gorton & Denton by-election, defeating Labour (political risk)
UK reportedly held talks with oil/gas firms on ending windfall tax
Today: MPC Member Pill speaks (14:00 Frankfurt Time)

JPY.name: JPY
JPY.summary: USD/JPY retreated amid early subdued risk appetite and following Tokyo CPI data, which printed firmer-than-expected but slowed from previous. Tokyo Core CPI fell below BoJ's 2% target for first time since October 2024 (1.8% vs 2.0%), yet yen firmed on data. Nikkei 225 traded indecisively amid firmer currency and mixed data releases.
drivers: Tokyo Core CPI fell below 2% target: 1.8% vs exp 1.7% (prev 2.0%), first time since Oct 2024
Tokyo CPI Ex Fresh Food & Energy 2.5% vs exp 2.3% (prev 2.4%), still elevated
Japanese Industrial Production (Jan) disappointed: 2.2% vs exp 5.3% (prev -0.1%)
Japanese Retail Sales (Jan) topped forecasts: 1.8% vs exp -0.4% (prev -0.9%)
Japan Finance Minister: monitoring yen decline with strong sense of urgency

AUD.name: AUD
AUD.summary: Antipodeans rebounded from prior day's trough but with further upside contained by mixed risk appetite and weaker CNH after PBoC actions to slow currency appreciation. ASX 200 mildly gained as strength in tech and telecoms atoned for losses in financials and consumer stocks. High-beta status pressured by NVIDIA selloff and tech weakness.
drivers: AUD rebounded from trough but upside capped by mixed risk appetite
PBoC cut FX Risk Reserve Ratio to 0% (from 20%), CNH weakened, weighs on AUD
ASX 200 mildly gained, tech/telecoms outperforming, financials/consumer stocks weak
NVIDIA post-earnings selloff (-5.5%) pressures high-beta currencies
Today: No major AUD data scheduled

CAD.name: CAD
CAD.summary: CAD pressured amid rangebound crude futures after whipsawing on US-Iran nuclear talks. Talks ended with no deal but 'significant progress', technical discussions resume Monday in Vienna. Critical Canadian GDP m/m data scheduled today at 14:30 Frankfurt Time. Canadian Minister Le Blanc said USMCA conversations not discouraging.
drivers: Crude futures rangebound after whipsawing on US-Iran talks (WTI settled -0.21 at $65.21/bbl)
US-Iran talks end with no deal but 'significant progress', technical talks resume Monday
Canadian Minister Le Blanc: USMCA conversations not discouraging
Trump-Xi summit preparations faltering, trade uncertainty persists
Today: GDP m/m (exp 0.2% vs prev 0.0%) - CRITICAL

CHF.name: CHF
CHF.summary: CHF mixed, benefiting from safe-haven flows amid US-Iran geopolitical tensions but pressured by EUR weakness. US-Iran talks ended with 'significant progress' but no deal, technical talks resume Monday in Vienna. Swiss GDP q/q and KOF Economic Barometer scheduled today.
drivers: Safe-haven flows support CHF amid US-Iran nuclear talks uncertainty
EUR weakness weighs on CHF crosses
US-Iran talks: significant progress but no deal, technical talks resume Monday
US President Trump to convene advisers Friday on Iran action
Today: GDP q/q (exp 0.2% vs prev -0.5%), KOF Economic Barometer (exp 103.0 vs prev 102.5)

NZD.name: NZD
NZD.summary: NZD rebounded from prior day's trough but with further upside contained by mixed risk appetite and weaker CNH after PBoC actions. High-beta status pressured by NVIDIA selloff and tech weakness. Antipodeans following broader risk sentiment amid choppy price action.
drivers: NZD rebounded from trough but upside capped by mixed risk appetite
PBoC cut FX Risk Reserve Ratio to 0%, CNH weakened, weighs on NZD
NVIDIA post-earnings selloff (-5.5%) pressures high-beta currencies
Trump-Xi summit preparations faltering, trade uncertainty persists
Today: No major NZD data scheduled

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: 10yr UST futures extended recent gains after advancing amid subdued risk appetite stateside and after better-than-previous 7yr auction. Little scheduled today aside from Core PCE and Chicago PMI data. Fed's Goolsbee stated rates can come down but does not want to front-load before inflation eases.
drivers: T-note futures extended gains on subdued risk appetite and tech weakness
Better-than-previous 7yr auction on Thursday
Fed's Goolsbee: rates can come down, but don't want to front-load before inflation eases
NVIDIA selloff (-5.5%) supports haven demand
Today: Core PCE Price Index m/m (exp 0.3% vs prev 0.2%), Chicago PMI

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bund futures remained firmer and approached just shy of 130.00 level. Attention turns to several releases from Germany, including CPI, Import Prices and Unemployment Rate. EU Commission eyes legal loophole to bypass Hungary's EUR 90bln loan veto (FT).
drivers: Bund futures firmer, approaching 130.00 level
Focus on German CPI (Feb), Import Prices (Jan), Unemployment Rate (Feb)
EU Commission eyes legal loophole to bypass Hungary's EUR 90bln loan veto (FT)
European equity futures flat after Thursday's -0.2% close
EUR/USD oscillating around 1.1800 level

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures continued to rebound from this week's low as participants digested Tokyo CPI data, which printed firmer-than-expected but slowed from previous. Tokyo Core CPI declined below BoJ's 2% target for first time since October 2024 (1.8% vs 2.0%). Little reaction to somewhat mixed 2yr JGB auction results.
drivers: JGB futures rebounded from week's low on Tokyo CPI data
Tokyo Core CPI fell below 2% target: 1.8% vs exp 1.7% (prev 2.0%), first time since Oct 2024
Little reaction to mixed 2yr JGB auction (b/c 3.32x vs prev 3.88x)
Japan Finance Minister: monitoring yen decline with strong sense of urgency
Nikkei 225 traded indecisively amid firmer currency

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts pressured following UK Green Party win in Gorton & Denton by-election, defeating PM Starmer's Labour. UK GfK Consumer Confidence fell to -19 (exp -15, prev -16), lowest level since November. Credit concerns persist after MFS collapse.
drivers: Gilts pressured on political risk: Green Party wins by-election, defeating Labour
UK GfK Consumer Confidence fell to -19 (exp -15, prev -16), lowest since November
Credit concerns persist after MFS collapse (fraud accusations)
GBP/USD languished near 1.3500 level
Today: MPC Member Pill speaks (14:00 Frankfurt Time)
